items.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
items.raw_content: As Anthropic forges a closer relationship with the state of California, the federal government has made an enemy out of the OpenAI rival.
items.summary: As Anthropic forges a closer relationship with the state of California, the federal government has made an enemy out of the OpenAI rival.

items.title: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
items.raw_content: A new proposal would ban the sale of Americans' health and location information to data brokers - including information people reveal to an AI chatbot like ChatGPT or Claude. In the coming weeks, Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) and Representative Mary Gay Scanlon (D-PA) are planning to debut a new version of the Health and Location […]

items.title: Vibe-coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
items.raw_content: Wix-owned vibe-coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model — with hopes that it will eventually outperform frontier models.
items.summary: Wix-owned vibe-coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model — with hopes that it will eventually outperform frontier models.

items.title: Arena, the AI leaderboard everyone uses, is now a $100M business
items.raw_content: The startup, which runs a popular free AI leaderboard, launched its commercial service just last September.
items.summary: The startup, which runs a popular free AI leaderboard, launched its commercial service just last September.

items.title: Cursor now has a mobile app for guiding your coding agent on the go
items.raw_content: Cursor has launched a new mobile app for remote oversight over coding agents.
items.summary: Cursor has launched a new mobile app for remote oversight over coding agents.

items.title: Crypto exchange OKX wants AI agents to hire and pay each other
items.raw_content: OKX is bringing together payments, identity, and reputation into a marketplace for AI agents.
items.summary: OKX is bringing together payments, identity, and reputation into a marketplace for AI agents.

items.title: Gemini’s personalized AI image generation is now free for US users
items.raw_content: Google is expanding Gemini’s personalized AI image generation to eligible free users in the U.S., allowing the chatbot to create images based on your interests and data from connected Google apps.
items.summary: Google is expanding Gemini’s personalized AI image generation to eligible free users in the U.S., allowing the chatbot to create images based on your interests and data from connected Google apps.

items.title: OpenAI is teasing new hardware… for Codex
items.raw_content: OpenAI is releasing some sort of device related to its AI-powered coding tool, Codex, on July 15th. In a video posted to X on Monday, OpenAI shows a square-shaped device with several buttons, alongside the caption, "Your favorite Codex shortcuts are getting an upgrade." This isn't the mysterious AI-powered device OpenAI is working on with […]

items.title: Lumo, Proton’s privacy-focused AI chatbot, gets an upgrade
items.raw_content: Proton's Lumo 2.0 is dropping this week, giving users a broader variety of capabilities.
items.summary: Proton's Lumo 2.0 is dropping this week, giving users a broader variety of capabilities.

items.title: The AI jobs debate just got messier
items.raw_content: A new report finds "high-intensity AI adopters” saw headcount increase 10.2%. Among those companies, entry-level headcount rose by 12%, countering the rhetoric that AI kills junior jobs.
items.summary: A new report finds "high-intensity AI adopters” saw headcount increase 10.2%. Among those companies, entry-level headcount rose by 12%, countering the rhetoric that AI kills junior jobs.

items.title: South Korean tech giants commit over $550B to ease ‘RAMageddon’
items.raw_content: The world's two largest memory chip companies vow to build more memory lab fabs as South Korea positions itself as an AI tech powerhouse country.
items.summary: The world's two largest memory chip companies vow to build more memory lab fabs as South Korea positions itself as an AI tech powerhouse country.

items.title: TIDAL cracks down on AI music by cutting off monetization
items.raw_content: In addition, TIDAL will use automated tools to remove AI-generated music that attempts to impersonate an artist or a group, the company said.
items.summary: In addition, TIDAL will use automated tools to remove AI-generated music that attempts to impersonate an artist or a group, the company said.

items.title: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music, but isn’t banning it outright
items.raw_content: Tidal shared its new policies regarding AI-generated music today and how the platform plans to "protect artists" and "inform listeners." Instead of banning it outright, starting on July 15th Tidal will label tracks it has identified as being 100 percent AI-generated with an icon. But starting today those tracks will no longer be monetizable. "Tidal's […]

items.title: China’s Z.ai claims it can match Mythos on cybersecurity
items.raw_content: China's Zhipu AI (Z.ai) released its open-weight GLM-5.2, and some researchers have claimed that it matches Mythos in certain bug-finding and cybersecurity scenarios. While GLM lags behind models from Anthropic and OpenAI in other, more general tasks, it seems that China has dramatically reduced the gap in the capabilities between its models and those of […]

items.title: Suno launches Spark incubator program to feed independent artists to its AI machine
items.raw_content: Suno has ambitions to be more than just a toy to churn out AI slop, it also wants to be a streaming destination and to break new artists. items.title: Prosecutors used ChatGPT logs as evidence in the Palisades fire trial
items.raw_content: Jonathan Rinderknecht was facing arson charges for setting a fire on New Year's Day in 2025, which became one of the deadliest wildfires in LA history. items.title: Margaret Atwood says the problem with AI is ‘garbage in, garbage out’

items.title: Why is Apple asking me to pay more for Big Tech’s AI obsession?
items.raw_content: Tim Cook recently said price increases were "unavoidable" and described the company's pricing as "unsustainable." The 16-inch MacBook Pro saw its price go up by $300. items.title: Anthropic’s Mythos 5 is back
